title: Ben Thompson is wrong: US frontier labs are right to be panicking
excerpt: American tech blogger Ben Thompson writes today that US frontier labs should not be panicking about Chinese open source models like Kimi K3, despite them being competitive with the best models from American labs. We can "distill" (ha!) his post down to two main arguments: Anthropic and OpenAI likely have among the lowest costs per unit of frontier-quality intelligence, thanks to model capability, serving scale, and token efficiency. The first is that US frontier labs are "likely" the cheapest
content_html:
ai

American tech blogger Ben Thompson writes today that US frontier labs should not be panicking about Chinese open source models like Kimi K3, despite them being competitive with the best models from American labs. We can "distill" (ha!) his post down to two main arguments:

Anthropic and OpenAI likely have among the lowest costs per unit of frontier-quality intelligence, thanks to model capability, serving scale, and token efficiency.

The first is that US frontier labs are "likely" the cheapest providers of inference and, because of that, what they lose in pricing power due to Chinese model competition, they can make up in volume.

It’s striking the extent to which Claude Code and Codex are proving to be quite sticky; whichever harness you start working with is likely to be the one you stick with, and that figures to be even more the case with non-technical users.

The second is that US frontier labs now own, and will continue to own, the user relationships because of the "stickiness" of their tools.

Thompson is wrong on both points.

Unit costs

Let's examine his first point about supposedly low US frontier labs unit costs. While US frontier labs likely have more chips than Chinese labs, everything else in their token supply chain: electricity, data center construction costs, etc, cost significantly more than the equivalent commodities in China today. And this is before Huawei's latest chips are shipping in volume.

Today, all Chinese unit cost inputs except chips are cheaper, so how likely is it that the US frontier labs have the lowest unit costs today? And as alternatives to US chips come online, like Huawei's latest chips, increasing market supply, how likely will it be that US frontier labs have the lowest unit costs in the future?

Stickiness

His second point, that tool stickiness will save US frontier labs is unconvincing to anyone who has actually been using AI instead of just writing about it. Take the examples he gives: "Claude Code" and "Codex." As any reader of Hacker News will tell you, Claude Code is already yesterday....in fact, it's last week. Yesterday was Codex, and Cursor is ancient history. Today, all the cool kids are using Opencode, which makes it trivial to switch between models and inference providers. But even when developers were still using Claude Code, they quickly figured out unapproved ways (often with Claude's help) use Anthropic's app with non-Anthropic, 3rd party models and inference providers. And often you couldn't even tell the difference.

And if this wasn't convincing enough: right now, literally as I write this, there's an article on the front page of Hacker News with over 200 comments telling us why we should all stop using Opencode with dozens of comments pitching Opencode alternatives. I've never heard of most of the suggestions, but I would be entirely unsurprised if one of them turns out to be the hottest, must-use tool that absolutely everyone in the developer community is using tomorrow.

The situation is the same with non-technical users who are seriously using AI. Today, they're using Claude Cowork but yesterday they were all using Manus. And before that, they were using Perplexity. These are professionals or prosumers trying to get an edge in their jobs or in their lives, they're willing to spend money and they jump ship as soon as something slightly better appears.

Sure, the mass market consumer user who asks "AI" a question a few times a week might have downloaded ChatGPT or Doubao when they first heard of AI and never switched to anything else. But those users usually don't pay much if anything at all and will hardly bail the labs out of their sinking ships.

Tool stickiness will save US frontier labs. Right.

US frontier labs are structurally screwed

Investors invested ungodly amounts of money in these labs because they told them they have some secret sauce and it turns out that the secret sauce was just ketchup. Everyone, even Chinese food delivery apps have the LLM ketchup. Not only do US frontier labs not have any secret sauce, they're building businesses where the largest input in their unit production is electricity in a country that has for decades been unable to build more electricity production. These US labs made massive capital commitments based on the assumption of future levels of profit that aren't ever going to come. And that's before you really dig down into the leveraged, financial house of cards they've built for themselves.

US frontier labs are right to be scared. And what do US businesses do when they're scared? Lobby for a government bailout.

I'm going to go out on a limb here (okay it's not a limb...it's more of bridge...a strong Chinese-made bridge, not one of those American ones that gets knocked down and never rebuilt) make three predictions:

  1. US frontier labs will not be saved by their unit costs or tool stickiness.
  2. US frontier labs will not be "frontier" for much longer.
  3. China will become the inference capital of the world: the highest quality, largest quantity and least expensive producer of tokens. This will happen over the next few years either in the absence of government regulations preventing US companies from using Chinese inference providers, or in spite of them.

Let's watch.

blocks.html_en: The second is that US frontier labs now own, and will continue to own, the user relationships because of the "stickiness" of their tools.

While US frontier labs likely have more chips than Chinese labs, everything else in their token supply chain: electricity, data center construction costs, etc, cost significantly more than the equivalent commodities in China today. And this is before Huawei's latest chips are shipping in volume.

sentences.en: And this is before Huawei's latest chips are shipping in volume.

sentences.en: And as alternatives to US chips come online, like Huawei's latest chips, increasing market supply, how likely will it be that US frontier labs have the lowest unit costs in the future?
sentences.zh: 而随着美国芯片的替代品上线，比如华为的最新芯片，市场供应量不断增加，美国前沿实验室未来单位成本最低的可能性有多大？

sentences.en: As any reader of Hacker News will tell you, Claude Code is already yesterday....in fact, it's last week.

sentences.en: Yesterday was Codex, and Cursor is ancient history.
sentences.zh: 昨天是 Codex，而 Cursor 是古代历史。

sentences.en: Today, all the cool kids are using Opencode, which makes it trivial to switch between models and inference providers.
sentences.zh: 如今，所有的酷孩子都在使用 Opencode，这使得在模型和推理提供程序之间切换变得微不足道。

sentences.en: But even when developers were still using Claude Code, they quickly figured out unapproved ways (often with Claude's help) use Anthropic's app with non-Anthropic, 3rd party models and inference providers.

sentences.en: And often you couldn't even tell the difference.
sentences.zh: 通常你甚至无法分辨出其中的区别。

blocks.html_en: And if this wasn't convincing enough: right now, literally as I write this, there's an article on the front page of Hacker News with over 200 comments telling us why we should all stop using Opencode with dozens of comments pitching Opencode alternatives. I've never heard of most of the suggestions, but I would be entirely unsurprised if one of them turns out to be the hottest, must-use tool that absolutely everyone in the developer community is using tomorrow.

sentences.en: And if this wasn't convincing enough: right now, literally as I write this, there's an article on the front page of Hacker News with over 200 comments telling us why we should all stop using Opencode with dozens of comments pitching Opencode alternatives.
sentences.zh: 如果这还不够令人信服的话：现在，就在我写这篇文章的时候，《黑客新闻》的头版上有一篇文章，其中有 200 多条评论告诉我们为什么我们应该停止使用 Opencode，其中有数十条评论提出 Opencode 替代方案。

sentences.en: I've never heard of most of the suggestions, but I would be entirely unsurprised if one of them turns out to be the hottest, must-use tool that absolutely everyone in the developer community is using tomorrow.
sentences.zh: 我从未听说过其中的大部分建议，但如果其中之一成为最热门、必须使用的工具，并且开发者社区中的每个人明天都会使用，我也不会感到惊讶。

blocks.html_en: The situation is the same with non-technical users who are seriously using AI. Today, they're using Claude Cowork but yesterday they were all using Manus. And before that, they were using Perplexity. These are professionals or prosumers trying to get an edge in their jobs or in their lives, they're willing to spend money and they jump ship as soon as something slightly better appears.

sentences.en: The situation is the same with non-technical users who are seriously using AI.
sentences.zh: 对于认真使用人工智能的非技术用户来说，情况也是如此。

sentences.en: Today, they're using Claude Cowork but yesterday they were all using Manus.
sentences.zh: 今天，他们使用 Claude Cowork，但昨天他​​们都使用 Manus。

sentences.en: And before that, they were using Perplexity.
sentences.zh: 在此之前，他们使用的是 Perplexity。

sentences.en: These are professionals or prosumers trying to get an edge in their jobs or in their lives, they're willing to spend money and they jump ship as soon as something slightly better appears.
sentences.zh: 这些人是专业人士或产消者，试图在工作或生活中获得优势，他们愿意花钱，一旦出现稍微好一点的东西，他们就会跳槽。

blocks.html_en: Sure, the mass market consumer user who asks "AI" a question a few times a week might have downloaded ChatGPT or Doubao when they first heard of AI and never switched to anything else. But those users usually don't pay much if anything at all and will hardly bail the labs out of their sinking ships.

sentences.en: Sure, the mass market consumer user who asks "AI" a question a few times a week might have downloaded ChatGPT or Doubao when they first heard of AI and never switched to anything else.
sentences.zh: 当然，每周问几次“人工智能”问题的大众市场消费者用户可能在第一次听说人工智能时就下载了 ChatGPT 或豆宝，并且从未转向其他任何产品。

sentences.en: But those users usually don't pay much if anything at all and will hardly bail the labs out of their sinking ships.
sentences.zh: 但这些用户通常不会支付太多费用，也很难将实验室从正在沉没的船只中拯救出来。

blocks.html_en: Tool stickiness will save US frontier labs. Right.

sentences.en: Tool stickiness will save US frontier labs.
sentences.zh: 工具粘性将拯救美国前沿实验室。

sentences.en: Right.
sentences.zh: 正确的。

sentences.en: US frontier labs are structurally screwed
sentences.zh: 美国前沿实验室结构混乱

These US labs made massive capital commitments based on the assumption of future levels of profit that aren't ever going to come. sentences.en: Investors invested ungodly amounts of money in these labs because they told them they have some secret sauce and it turns out that the secret sauce was just ketchup.

sentences.en: Everyone, even Chinese food delivery apps have the LLM ketchup.
sentences.zh: 每个人，甚至中国的食品配送应用程序都有法学硕士番茄酱。

sentences.en: Not only do US frontier labs not have any secret sauce, they're building businesses where the largest input in their unit production is electricity in a country that has for decades been unable to build more electricity production.
sentences.zh: 美国前沿实验室不仅没有任何秘密武器，而且在一个几十年来无法建立更多电力生产的国家，他们正在建立单位生产中最大投入是电力的企业。

sentences.en: These US labs made massive capital commitments based on the assumption of future levels of profit that aren't ever going to come.
sentences.zh: 这些美国实验室基于对未来永远不会实现的利润水平的假设，做出了大量资本承诺。

sentences.en: And that's before you really dig down into the leveraged, financial house of cards they've built for themselves.
sentences.zh: 那是在你真正深入研究他们为自己建造的杠杆金融纸牌屋之前。

blocks.html_en: US frontier labs are right to be scared. And what do US businesses do when they're scared? Lobby for a government bailout.

sentences.en: US frontier labs are right to be scared.
sentences.zh: 美国前沿实验室的恐惧是有道理的。

sentences.en: And what do US businesses do when they're scared?
sentences.zh: 当美国企业感到害怕时会做什么？

sentences.en: Lobby for a government bailout.
sentences.zh: 游说政府救助。

blocks.html_en: I'm going to go out on a limb here (okay it's not a limb...it's more of bridge...a strong Chinese-made bridge, not one of those American ones that gets knocked down and never rebuilt) make three predictions:

sentences.en: I'm going to go out on a limb here (okay it's not a limb...it's more of bridge...a strong Chinese-made bridge, not one of those American ones that gets knocked down and never rebuilt) make three predictions:
sentences.zh: 我要在这里冒险（好吧，这不是一个肢体......它更像是一座桥......一座坚固的中国制造的桥梁，而不是那些被推倒且从未重建的美国桥梁）做出三个预测：

blocks.html_en: • US frontier labs will not be saved by their unit costs or tool stickiness.

sentences.en: US frontier labs will not be saved by their unit costs or tool stickiness.
sentences.zh: 美国前沿实验室不会因其单位成本或工具粘性而得到拯救。

blocks.html_en: • US frontier labs will not be "frontier" for much longer.

sentences.en: US frontier labs will not be "frontier" for much longer.
sentences.zh: 美国前沿实验室的“前沿”地位不会持续太久。

blocks.html_en: • China will become the inference capital of the world: the highest quality, largest quantity and least expensive producer of tokens. This will happen over the next few years either in the absence of government regulations preventing US companies from using Chinese inference providers, or in spite of them.

sentences.en: China will become the inference capital of the world: the highest quality, largest quantity and least expensive producer of tokens.
sentences.zh: 中国将成为世界的推理之都：质量最高、数量最多、成本最低的代币生产国。

sentences.en: This will happen over the next few years either in the absence of government regulations preventing US companies from using Chinese inference providers, or in spite of them.
sentences.zh: 这种情况在未来几年内将会发生，要么是在没有政府法规阻止美国公司使用中国推理提供商的情况下，要么是尽管​​有政府法规。

blocks.html_en: Let's watch.

sentences.en: Let's watch.
sentences.zh: 让我们拭目以待。

content_html_zh:
ai

美国科技博主 Ben Thompson 今天写道，美国前沿实验室不应该对像 Kimi K3 这样的中国开源模型感到恐慌，尽管它们与美国实验室的最佳模型具有竞争力。 我们可以“蒸馏”（哈！） 他的帖子主要有两个论点：

由于模型功能、服务规模和代币效率，Anthropic 和 OpenAI 可能是每单位前沿质量智能成本最低的。

首先，美国前沿实验室“可能”是最便宜的推理提供者，正因为如此，他们因中国模型竞争而失去的定价能力，可以通过数量来弥补。

事实证明，克劳德法典和法典的粘性程度令人震惊。无论您开始使用哪一种工具，您都可能会坚持使用，对于非技术用户来说更是如此。

其次，美国前沿实验室现在拥有并将继续拥有用户关系，因为他们的工具具有“粘性”。

汤普森在这两点上都是错误的。

单位成本

让我们来看看他关于所谓的美国前沿实验室单位成本较低的第一点。 虽然美国前沿实验室可能比中国实验室拥有更多的芯片，但其代币供应链中的其他所有内容：电力、数据中心建设成本等，其成本远远高于当今中国的同等商品。 这是在华为最新芯片批量出货之前。

如今，除了芯片之外，中国所有的单位成本投入都更便宜，那么美国前沿实验室今天单位成本最低的可能性有多大呢？ 而随着美国芯片的替代品上线，比如华为的最新芯片，市场供应量不断增加，美国前沿实验室未来单位成本最低的可能性有多大？

粘性

他的第二点，即工具粘性将拯救美国前沿实验室，对于任何真正使用人工智能而不是仅仅写下人工智能的人来说，是无法令人信服的。 以他给出的例子为例：“克劳德法典”和“法典”。 正如《黑客新闻》的任何读者都会告诉您的那样，克劳德代码已经是昨天了......事实上，这是上周。 昨天是 Codex，而 Cursor 是古代历史。 如今，所有的酷孩子都在使用 Opencode，这使得在模型和推理提供程序之间切换变得微不足道。 但即使开发人员仍在使用 Claude 代码，他们也很快找到了未经批准的方式（通常在 Claude 的帮助下）将 Anthropic 的应用程序与非 Anthropic、第 3 方模型和推理提供程序一起使用。 通常你甚至无法分辨出其中的区别。

如果这还不够令人信服的话：现在，就在我写这篇文章的时候，《黑客新闻》的头版上有一篇文章，其中有 200 多条评论告诉我们为什么我们应该停止使用 Opencode，其中有数十条评论提出 Opencode 替代方案。 我从未听说过其中的大部分建议，但如果其中之一成为最热门、必须使用的工具，并且开发者社区中的每个人明天都会使用，我也不会感到惊讶。

对于认真使用人工智能的非技术用户来说，情况也是如此。 今天，他们使用 Claude Cowork，但昨天他​​们都使用 Manus。 在此之前，他们使用的是 Perplexity。 这些人是专业人士或产消者，试图在工作或生活中获得优势，他们愿意花钱，一旦出现稍微好一点的东西，他们就会跳槽。

当然，每周问几次“人工智能”问题的大众市场消费者用户可能在第一次听说人工智能时就下载了 ChatGPT 或豆宝，并且从未转向其他任何产品。 但这些用户通常不会支付太多费用，也很难将实验室从正在沉没的船只中拯救出来。

工具粘性将拯救美国前沿实验室。 正确的。

美国前沿实验室结构混乱

投资者在这些实验室投入了巨额资金，因为他们告诉他们他们有一些秘密酱汁，但事实证明，秘密酱汁只是番茄酱。 每个人，甚至中国的食品配送应用程序都有法学硕士番茄酱。 美国前沿实验室不仅没有任何秘密武器，而且在一个几十年来无法建立更多电力生产的国家，他们正在建立单位生产中最大投入是电力的企业。 这些美国实验室基于对未来永远不会实现的利润水平的假设，做出了大量资本承诺。 那是在你真正深入研究他们为自己建造的杠杆金融纸牌屋之前。

美国前沿实验室的恐惧是有道理的。 当美国企业感到害怕时会做什么？ 游说政府救助。

我要在这里冒险（好吧，这不是一个肢体......它更像是一座桥......一座坚固的中国制造的桥梁，而不是那些被推倒且从未重建的美国桥梁）做出三个预测：

  1. 美国前沿实验室不会因其单位成本或工具粘性而得到拯救。
  2. 美国前沿实验室的“前沿”地位不会持续太久。
  3. 中国将成为世界的推理之都：质量最高、数量最多、成本最低的代币生产国。 这种情况在未来几年内将会发生，要么是在没有政府法规阻止美国公司使用中国推理提供商的情况下，要么是尽管​​有政府法规。

让我们拭目以待。
